slides.title: Kissflow Integrations
slides.subtitle: Connect Kissflow with internal and external systems
slides.notes: Source: academy.kissflow.com Integrations course, community Integrations guide

slides.title: Integration Landscape
bullets: Pre-built connectors for popular SaaS applications
REST API for custom system integration
Webhooks for real-time event-driven automation
Kissflow-to-Kissflow cross-account data exchange
Connector Builder for custom connectors (Enterprise)
Cryptography connector for data encryption/decryption
slides.notes: Overview of all integration options available.

slides.title: Pre-Built Connectors
bullets: Ready-made connectors in Kissflow Integration marketplace
Categories: CRM, ERP, HR, Finance, IT, Communication, Storage
Configure authentication (OAuth, API key, basic auth)
Map fields between Kissflow and external system
Bi-directional sync where supported
No code required for standard connector configurations
slides.notes: Browse integration catalog in admin or app settings.

slides.title: Connector Builder
bullets: Build custom connectors without leaving Kissflow
Define authentication methods for target system
Configure actions (API calls Kissflow makes) and triggers (events that start flows)
Test connector in sandbox before production use
Enterprise plan feature — announced June 2026
Empowers IT teams to integrate proprietary systems

slides.title: Creating Triggers
bullets: Trigger = event or condition that starts an integration
Acts as listener waiting for specific change in connected system
Examples: new record created, status changed, file uploaded
Map trigger payload to Kissflow process or dataform fields
Enterprise plan — Connector builder documentation
Combine multiple triggers for complex event handling

slides.title: Integration Actions
bullets: Actions execute when integration runs — create, read, update, delete
Use in workflow steps: 'On approval, create record in Salesforce'
Chain multiple actions in sequence
Error handling and retry configuration
Log integration execution for troubleshooting
Test mode to validate without affecting production data
slides.notes: Demo: on PO approval → create entry in ERP system.

slides.title: Cryptography Connector
bullets: In-built security tool for data protection in integrations
Encrypt sensitive data before sending to external systems
Decrypt incoming data for use in Kissflow forms
Supports industry-standard encryption algorithms
Announced June 2026 — Product announcement
Critical for PII, financial, and healthcare data handling

slides.title: REST API Integration
bullets: Kissflow REST API for programmatic access to all platform data
Authentication via API keys and service accounts
CRUD operations on process items, dataform records, users
Webhook subscriptions for outbound event notifications
Rate limits and pagination for large data sets
Full reference at api.kissflow.com

slides.title: Webhooks
bullets: Outbound webhooks notify external systems of Kissflow events
Trigger on: item created, approved, rejected, completed, field changed
Payload includes item data and event metadata
Configure endpoint URL, headers, and authentication
Retry logic for failed webhook deliveries
Use for real-time sync with external databases and systems

slides.title: Integration in Workflows
bullets: Add integration step in workflow designer
Execute connector action at specific workflow stage
Conditional integration — only run when field values match
Map form fields to integration input parameters
Store integration response in form fields for later steps
Error branch — route to manual review if integration fails
slides.notes: Most common pattern: approval triggers external system update.

slides.title: Integrations Best Practices
bullets: Start with pre-built connectors before building custom ones
Use service accounts with minimum required permissions
Test in sandbox environment before production deployment
Log and monitor integration executions regularly
Document field mappings and integration architecture
Plan error handling — what happens when external system is down?
